event_detail: Donald Trump inaugurated as 47th President, executive orders on trade policy expected
25% on Canada/Mexico, 10% on China under IEEPA emergency authority
China announces 15% counter-tariffs on US agricultural products
Global 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum imports
25% tariffs on Canada and Mexico take effect
EU retaliates with tariffs on US bourbon, motorcycles, agricultural products
Fed Chair Powell says tariff inflation likely transitory, one-time price adjustment
Sweeping reciprocal tariffs announced: 10% baseline + country-specific rates up to 49%
Powell acknowledges tariff impact may be larger and more persistent than expected
90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for all countries except China; 10% baseline remains
Tit-for-tat escalation reaches 125% US tariffs on China, 84% China on US
25% tariffs on imported automobiles take effect under Section 232
US-China Geneva negotiations: mutual tariff reduction, China from 125% to 10% for 90 days
Tax Policy Center publishes income quintile burden analysis showing regressive impact
Original 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs expires, rates revert or renegotiate
25% tariffs on copper imports take effect under Section 232
Powell characterizes tariff inflation as one-time price level shift, not persistent inflation
Federal Circuit Court rules IEEPA does not authorize tariffs, legal challenge escalates
Multiple manufacturers announce US factory investment commitments totaling $50B+ (Reshoring Initiative tracker)
US-China Busan negotiations: tariffs reduced to 35-50% range on most goods
BLS reports manufacturing employment down ~83K since Jan 2025 baseline despite reshoring announcements
Powell acknowledges very unusual economy; Fed delivers 3rd rate cut of 2025
Full-year data shows US trade deficit widened despite tariffs — deficit paradox confirmed
Supreme Court agrees to hear challenge to IEEPA tariff authority
Supreme Court rules 6-3 that IEEPA does not authorize broad tariff authority
Congress passes Section 122 authority as temporary replacement, 150-day window
Coalition of 24 state attorneys general files lawsuit challenging Section 122 tariff authority
USTR launches Section 301 investigations as longer-term tariff mechanism
Section 232 tariffs up to 100% on patented pharmaceuticals; tiered rates for EU/Japan/Korea/Switzerland (15%); UK exempt; effective Jul 31
Section 122 tariffs in effect at 10%, effective tariff rate 11.0% (highest since 1943). July 24 expiration approaching. Pharma S232 pending Jul 31.
Section 122 authority expires — Congress must act, extend, or let tariffs lapse. Rate drops to ~8.2%
